lede: A look at March 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Belle (singer) and Nicolas Lombaerts, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Taliban
events.desc: Four suspected Taliban members attack the Kabul Serena Hotel, killing at least nine people.

events.title: Eyjafjallajökull
events.desc: Eyjafjallajökull in Iceland begins eruptions that would last for three months, heavily disrupting air travel in Europe.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: A Provisional IRA bomb kills two children in Warrington, England. It leads to mass protests in both Britain and Ireland.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: General Douglas MacArthur, at Terowie, South Australia, makes his famous speech regarding the fall of the Philippines, in which he says: "I came out of Bataan and I shall return".

events.title: Chiang Kai-shek
events.desc: Chiang Kai-shek initiates a purge of communist elements within the National Revolutionary Army in Guangzhou.

events.title: Arts Club of Chicago
events.desc: The Arts Club of Chicago hosts the opening of Pablo Picasso's first United States showing, entitled Original Drawings by Pablo Picasso, becoming an early proponent of modern art in the United States.

events.title: Republican Party (United States)
events.desc: The Republican Party of the United States is organized in Ripon, Wisconsin, US.

events.title: Harriet Beecher Stowe
events.desc: Harriet Beecher Stowe's Uncle Tom's Cabin is published.

events.title: Great Boston Fire of 1760
events.desc: The Great Boston Fire of 1760 destroys 349 buildings.

births.name: Peter Truscott, Baron Truscott
births.line: British Labour Party politician and peer

births.name: Ferris Jacobs Jr.
births.line: Jr., American general, lawyer, and politician (died 1886)

deaths.text: George Curzon, 1st Marquess Curzon of Kedleston, English politician, 35th Governor-General of India

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed John of Parma
Christian feast day: María Josefa Sancho de Guerra
